description: Authoritative Rhineland glyph contract for the Hildegard Neumes font project. Single source of truth for glyph names, advance widths, SMuFL cross-references, and documentation. Consumed by scripts/generate-rhena-glyphs.py to emit the Rust Glyph table for Rhena adoption. 2026-04-14 width review corrected three overflow bugs: rh_virga=90, rh_liquescent_asc=160, rh_liquescent_desc=160 (docs/planning/width-review-2026-04-14.md). 2026-04-19 SMuFL alignment (docs/adr/ADR-0009-smufl-alignment.md) bumped two tradition-agnostic geometric widths to match Bravura conventions: rh_divisio_finalis=120, rh_virgula=91. 2026-04-19 paleographic correction: rh_virga=240 (was 90) — direct manuscript comparison confirmed virga head ≈ punctum breadth. Calligraphic atoms retain Rhineland-distinct widths per ADR-0008. Units are design units (du); 1 staff space = 250 du in our 1000-UPM scale.

coordinate_system.comment: Font authored Y-up; Rhena's SVG backend flips to Y-down at render time via a transform. See hildegard-neumes rhena_integration_plan.md § 3.3.

glyphs.doc: Rhineland punctum: calligraphic parallelogram mark; basic single note.

Rhineland virga: calligraphic punctum-breadth head + thin descending stem + small rightward foot hook. Head shape and width match rh_punctum; stem descends from head's right side. Width 240 du set 2026-04-19 after direct manuscript comparison (superseded earlier 90).
Small tilted diamond for climacus descending notes.
Wavy ornamental ascending note; 2–3 rounded calligraphic humps.
Ornamental repercussion note; small punctum body with a wavy tail. Directional split deferred to v2.
Apostropha head; repeated by the renderer for bistropha and tristropha.
Rhineland pressus: vertical line attached to a short undulating line (Gardiner p. 94). No SMuFL equivalent.
Ascending liquescent terminal; small head + upward calligraphic tail. Lighter stroke per Gardiner p. 92.
Descending liquescent terminal; small head + downward calligraphic tail.
Small diminutive punctum for diminutive-liquescent positions.
Rhineland movable C clef: horizontal dash above a calligraphic C.
Rhineland F clef for low-range antiphons.
Short phrase break; thin bar spanning one staff space.
Medium phrase break; thin bar spanning the full 4-line staff.
Long phrase break; thin bar extending above and below the staff.
Final double bar; two equal-weight 16-du verticals separated by an 88-du gap (total 120 du). SMuFL-aligned 2026-04-19.
Breath mark above the top staff line. SMuFL-aligned to Bravura convention (width 91 du, y ∈ [+255, +500]) 2026-04-19.
Ascending stroke connector for pes/scandicus joins. Private, no SMuFL.
Descending diagonal connector for flexa/clivis. Private, no SMuFL.
